__fixture__.name: admin_policy_bundle_fail_closed_signature_verification_failed
__fixture__.scenario: A signature-verification failure on the admin_policy_bundle triggers the fail_closed_for_privileged_operations corruption posture. Privileged operations (policy apply, admin-audit write, signed-bundle refresh) refuse to run until a fresh signed epoch is distributed. Non-privileged editor operations continue under open-with-warning semantics so the user can still save work.

state_segments.note: The new policy bundle failed signature verification. Privileged operations refuse until a fresh signed epoch is distributed; the last-known-good bundle continues to govern editing.
Non-privileged editor operations continue under open-with-warning semantics so the user can save work while privileged operations refuse.

downgrade_reasons.note: Policy narrows to the last-known-good epoch until the signed distribution path re-establishes trust.

failure_states.note: Privileged operations (policy apply, admin-audit write, signed-bundle refresh) refuse; the support bundle includes the candidate bundle's failure evidence for the admin authority.

preserved_prior_artifacts.note: Last-known-good policy bundle held by the admin authority is projected here as the preservation reference. The client does not author a local backup of signed bundles.

notes: Fail-closed-for-privileged-operations posture: signed admin authority refuses to act until trust is re-established; editing continues under warning so user work is preserved.
